Cath: 1.  Coronary angiography in this right dominant system demonstrated three vessel coronary artery disease
The LMCA was a long vessel with mild plaquing, and proximal 30% and distal 20% lesions.
.  The LAD had a 30-40% ostial tubular lesion.  The LAD and the diagonal were calcified. The LAD after the takeoff of D1 had a 95% stenosis with TIMI 2 flow
A previously large D1 was totally occluded with distal filling via LCx collaterals and 40% in-stent restenosis.

The LCx had minimal luminal irregularities and supplied the atrial branch, OM1, 2 LPL's (proximal 50% stenosis at LPL1), and collaterals to the RPDA. The ramus has an ostial 30% and proximal 80% then 50% lesions with diffuse plaquing. Multiple distal branches are noted.
The RCA has a proximal 30% stenosis and a mid 90% stenosis with haziness suggestive of thrombus with TIMI 1 flow distally.
The RPDA has a mid 80% lesion at 2 major sidebranches follwed by a 90% stenosi revealed minimally elevated left sided filling pressures with LVEDP of 13 mmHg
Left ventriculography revealed no mitral regurgitation.
The LVEF was calculated to be 45-49% with posterobasal, inferior and anterolateral hypokinesis and inferoapical akinesis.
Echo: Pre Procedure: The left atrium is mildly dilated. No mass/thrombus is seen in the left atrium or left atrial appendage. No atrial septal defect is seen by 2D or color Doppler.
The left ventricular cavity size is normal. Regional left ventricular wall motion is normal. Overall left ventricular systolic function is normal (LVEF>55%).
Right ventricular chamber size and free wall motion are normal.
There are simple atheroma in the aortic arch. There are simple atheroma in the descending thoracic aorta.
The aortic valve leaflets (3) appear structurally normal with good leaflet excursion
no aortic regurgitation. There is no aortic valve stenosis. No aortic regurgitation is seen.
The mitral valve leaflets are mildly thickened.
Mild (1+) mitral regurgitation is seen. There is no pericardial effusion
Post Off Pump Bypass:
Wall motion and LV function unchanged. Aortic contours preserved. Mitral regurgitation appears trace to mild. Remaining exam unchanged
CXR: 1. Stable small-to-moderate right apical pneumothorax.
Persistent bibasilar atelectasis.
Slight improved aeration of the left lung base.
NSTEMI
showed severe three vessel coronary artery disease.
. He underwent a Cardiac catheterization on [**2106-8-11**] which showed severe three vessel coronary artery disease.
referred
for surgical revascularization.
he was brought to the	 operating room,
underwent an off-pump coronary artery bypass graft x 4
was transferred to the CSRU
in stable condition.
weaned from sedation
, awoke neurologically intact
extubated
started on Imdur
radial artery graft
and his drips were weaned
started on beta blockers and diuretics and gently diuresed towards his pre-op weight
was transferred to SDU
three his epicardial pacing wires and chest tubes removed.
Chest x-ray following removal of chest tubes revealed a small apical pneumothorax
. Chest x-ray the following day showed the pneumothorax to have decreased in size.
worked with physical therapy for strength and mobility
